response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer (~13 years) with strengths in TypeScript/Node.js, D, testing, and distributed web systems, but the role is a Principal-level hardware performance architecture position centered on SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator and GPU architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. There is essentially no evidence of these competencies. The only adjacent items are some low-latency D microservices and brief C/C++ work at National Instruments, which fall far short of the required silicon-level modeling and AI accelerator expertise. The BS-only education would also require demonstrated equivalent hardware-architecture experience that is absent here.
strengths: Deep, long-tenured software engineering experience across many domains
Some low-latency systems work in D (async I/O, fibers, high-perf databases)
Brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and embedded/robotics tinkering
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI-accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Education/experience profile does not map to a Principal hardware-architect IC role
